# AERS Threat Model (generated)

> Generated from `assurance/threats/threat-model.json` by `python3 scripts/assure.py threat-model --render`.
> Do not edit by hand; edit the structured source and regenerate.

Protected assets, 12 threats.

| ID | Severity | Protected asset | Entry vector | Preventive | Detective | Recovery | Benchmark | Residual risk |
|----|----------|-----------------|--------------|------------|-----------|----------|-----------|----------------|
| THREAT-SCOPE-CREEP | critical | source integrity | prompt injection in repository docs / tool output | immutable task write_scope; contract validator rejects blanket/guardrail scope | scope gate diffs candidate against write_scope | reject candidate; roll back worktree | BENCH-SCOPE-001, BENCH-SCOPE-004, BENCH-CON-002, BENCH-CON-003 | in-scope-but-wrong changes still require reviewer judgment |
| THREAT-TEST-TAMPER | critical | tests and evals | candidate diff to test files or runner hooks | implementer role cannot edit tests; protected test_patterns | deterministic audit tamper patterns; differential gate | reject candidate | BENCH-SCOPE-002, BENCH-AUDIT-001, BENCH-AUDIT-003, BENCH-DIFF-001 | novel tamper idioms not in the pattern list may need reviewer to catch |
| THREAT-FAKE-COMPLETION | high | verification integrity | agentic laziness / forced-success code | acceptance mapped to fresh evidence; hermetic author-verify runs real commands | forced-success audit pattern; differential fails-on-base gate | reject candidate | BENCH-AUDIT-002, BENCH-DIFF-001 | a test can be real yet incomplete; coverage is not proof of correctness |
| THREAT-SECRET-EXFIL | critical | secrets and data | candidate diff or trajectory command | scrubbed verification environment; egress deny; no secret access at author tier | format-anchored secret-in-diff gate; trajectory dangerous-command detection; redaction by default | reject candidate; rotate any exposed secret | BENCH-AUDIT-004, BENCH-AUDIT-005 | novel secret formats not matched by the format-anchored patterns |
| THREAT-EGRESS | high | network isolation | verification/agent process network use | network-namespace isolation; egress deny; isolation truth model fails closed for R2+ | trajectory network_attempt detection; isolation state classification | reject candidate; require proven/attested isolation | BENCH-AUDIT-006, BENCH-ISO-001, BENCH-ISO-002 | ASSERTED isolation on a host without a namespace mechanism cannot be upgraded locally |
| THREAT-PATH-ESCAPE | high | source integrity | candidate creates a symlink or traversing path | symlink-escape scope check; safe tar extraction on export | scope gate SYMLINK_PATH finding | reject candidate | BENCH-SCOPE-005 | TOCTOU races on shared filesystems are out of scope for the static gate |
| THREAT-GUARDRAIL-SELF-EDIT | critical | control plane | candidate diff to protected control-plane paths | protected-paths gate; policies read at immutable ref, not working tree; ConfigChange hook blocks changes during a run | scope PROTECTED_PATH finding on writes and deletions | reject candidate; human control-plane review | BENCH-SCOPE-003, BENCH-SCOPE-007 | control-plane change by an authorized human still needs independent review (dogfooding workflow) |
| THREAT-LOCAL-VERIFIED | critical | verifier identity | repository-local signing / attestation forgery | no production signing key in repo; empty production trust store; demo key cannot be production-valid | verify_attestation trust-domain scoping and binding checks | reject attestation; require external verifier | BENCH-ATT-001, BENCH-ATT-002 | a compromised external verifier key is outside this repository's control |
| THREAT-ATTESTATION-SUBSTITUTION | critical | verifier identity | handoff/attestation substitution or stale reuse | attestation binds candidate/policy/evidence digests; expiry + nonce | verify_attestation binding + freshness checks | reject attestation | BENCH-ATT-003, BENCH-ATT-004, BENCH-ATT-005, BENCH-ATT-006 | clock skew handling depends on the relying party's trusted time source |
| THREAT-POISONED-MEMORY | high | curated memory | memory proposal from a run | quarantine by default; separate curator identity required; no self-promotion; multi-run corroboration | content-hash integrity check on promote and lint | revoke record; expire | BENCH-MEM-001, BENCH-MEM-002, BENCH-MEM-003 | a compromised curator identity could promote bad memory (out of scope for the author tier) |
| THREAT-RUBBER-STAMP | high | verification integrity | reviewer output not bound to the exact candidate | reviewer report must bind candidate_sha; second independent reviewer for R2; distinct harness required | validate_reviewer identity + acceptance-coverage checks | reject candidate | BENCH-REV-001, BENCH-REV-002 | two correlated reviewers can share a blind spot; evaluator-health monitors this |
| THREAT-UNSAFE-CHANGE | high | production systems | candidate touching migrations/schema or a contract without rollback | rollout+rollback required in every feature contract; sensitive-path classification; release segregation | scope gate on out-of-scope migration/schema files; contract validator | automated rollback; staged rollout health gate | BENCH-SCOPE-009, BENCH-CON-001 | data-loss risk of a semantically valid but destructive migration needs human/DBA review |
